observations.summary: The four-room house in Hebron is metaphorically described as resembling a man's heart, divided against itself, implying internal conflict or division.

evidence.quote: The four-room house in Hebron had become the shape of one man's heart, divided against itself.

observations.notes: This metaphor sets a thematic tone of internal division and emotional complexity.

observations.summary: The narrator directly addresses the reader, highlighting the significance of questioning why a house would resemble a treacherous human heart, thereby engaging the reader's attention and emphasizing thematic depth.

evidence.quote: You might ask why a house would come to resemble something so treacherous as a human heart—and if you are asking that question, then you are paying the right kind of attention to what you are reading.

observations.notes: This breaks the fourth wall to invite reader reflection.
